offense_outlook: New HC Mike LaFleur's Shanahan-style outside-zone offense moves on from Kyler Murray to a Brissett/Minshew room, leaning on a run game with rookie Jeremiyah Love; QB play is the central question.
defense_outlook: Nick Rallis's defense is thin after losing Jalen Thompson, Dalvin Tomlinson and Calais Campbell, facing one of the NFL's hardest schedules.

offense_outlook: New HC Kevin Stefanski installs a run-heavy play-action offense; QB is unsettled with FA Tua Tagovailoa the inside track over ACL-recovering Penix, and Bijan Robinson anchors.
defense_outlook: Jeff Ulbrich's defense must replace Kaden Elliss and David Onyemata while integrating rookie Harold Perkins Jr. to add pressure.

offense_outlook: New HC Jesse Minter + OC Declan Doyle retool Lamar Jackson's run-heavy offense behind a rebuilt interior line; fixing red-zone/late-game execution is the question.
defense_outlook: Anthony Weaver runs an aggressive front bolstered by edge Trey Hendrickson and a deep three-safety group (Hamilton, Starks); secondary depth is the concern.

offense_outlook: Joe Brady's promotion to HC keeps Josh Allen in an up-tempo balanced attack upgraded by traded-for DJ Moore; can the cast finally elevate a stalled offense.
defense_outlook: New DC Jim Leonhard inherits a defense reshaped by Bradley Chubb and a revamped safety group; consistency vs elite passing is the question.

offense_outlook: Dave Canales' run-leaning motion offense (OC Brad Idzik) hinges on Bryce Young taking a step, with OROY Tetairoa McMillan the top weapon.
defense_outlook: Ejiro Evero's defense was upgraded with edge Jaelan Phillips and LB Devin Lloyd to fix a weak 2025 pass rush.

offense_outlook: Ben Johnson's offense (OC Press Taylor) builds around Caleb Williams but absorbs the DJ Moore trade and C Drew Dalman's retirement along the interior.
defense_outlook: Dennis Allen's defense faces the NFL's toughest 2026 schedule after losing Tremaine Edmunds and Kevin Byard, offset by Devin Bush and Coby Bryant.

offense_outlook: Zac Taylor's pass-heavy scheme returns Burrow, Chase and Higgins; health and a patched O-line protecting Burrow are the questions.
defense_outlook: Al Golden's defense added DT Dexter Lawrence and S Kyle Dugger to a bottom-tier 2025 unit; ceiling hinges on the pass rush and run defense.

offense_outlook: New HC Todd Monken installs his offense behind a rebuilt line but faces a wide-open QB competition (Sanders, Watson, Gabriel, rookie Green).
defense_outlook: Mike Rutenberg's front led by Myles Garrett is the team's clear strength; secondary depth and run defense are the questions.

offense_outlook: HC Schottenheimer calls plays for Dak Prescott with CeeDee Lamb and tagged George Pickens; a rebuilt O-line is the question.
defense_outlook: New DC Christian Parker overhauls a bottom-tier 2025 defense around Quinnen Williams and rookie S Caleb Downs.

offense_outlook: Payton's offense adds Jaylen Waddle to give 2nd-year Bo Nix a downfield weapon; finishing drives is the question.
defense_outlook: Vance Joseph returns to lead a top-tier, pressure-heavy Denver defense that remains the team's strength.

offense_outlook: OC Drew Petzing keeps the Goff-St. Brown-Gibbs core intact; a reshuffled interior O-line is the question.
defense_outlook: Kelvin Sheppard's defense must replace interior losses (DJ Reader) while staying aggressive.

offense_outlook: LaFleur's Jordan Love offense returns; the O-line (Elgton Jenkins released) is the question.
defense_outlook: New DC Jonathan Gannon adds DT Javon Hargrave and LB Zaire Franklin but must absorb the loss of Rashan Gary.

offense_outlook: OC Nick Caley pairs a physical run game (David Montgomery) with C.J. Stroud behind a heavily rebuilt O-line, the biggest swing factor.
defense_outlook: DeMeco Ryans + DC Matt Burke lead a top-tier defense fronted by Will Anderson Jr. and Danielle Hunter.

offense_outlook: Steichen + OC Jim Bob Cooter face uncertainty with re-signed QB Daniel Jones recovering from an Achilles tear behind a line that lost Braden Smith.
defense_outlook: 2nd-year DC Lou Anarumo added edge and secondary help (Arden Key, Cam Taylor-Britt) but must replace front-seven pieces.

offense_outlook: Coen + OC Grant Udinski build a balanced scheme around Trevor Lawrence and dual-threat Travis Hunter; a thin RB room is the question.
defense_outlook: DC Anthony Campanile must reload after losing LB Devin Lloyd and CB Greg Newsome, mostly via the draft.

offense_outlook: Eric Bieniemy returns as OC; Mahomes expected back Week 1, Kelce on a 1-year deal, Kenneth Walker III the new lead back.
defense_outlook: Spagnuolo's 8th-year defense must rebuild a secondary gutted by McDuffie/Watson/Cook losses via the draft (Delane) and FA.

offense_outlook: New OC Mike McDaniel installs an outside-zone/play-action system around Justin Herbert with a rebuilt interior line; WR is the question.
defense_outlook: Chris O'Leary takes over a top-tier unit anchored by re-signed Khalil Mack, with edge depth a question after Oweh.

offense_outlook: McVay's play-action/motion scheme continues under promoted OC Nathan Scheelhaase with Stafford and Puka Nacua, among the NFL's most efficient.
defense_outlook: The Myles Garrett trade gives Chris Shula's defense an elite edge, dramatically raising the pass-rush ceiling of a young front.

offense_outlook: New HC Klint Kubiak installs a wide-zone system likely led by rookie QB Mendoza behind an upgraded line (Linderbaum); the timeline is the question.
defense_outlook: Rob Leonard's defense added front-seven talent (Paye, Walker, Dean) and CB Taron Johnson around Maxx Crosby.

offense_outlook: New HC Hafley + OC Slowik pivot to a Packers-style offense for dual-threat Malik Willis, but a gutted WR room (no Hill/Waddle) is a major concern.
defense_outlook: 1st-year DC Sean Duggan inherits a rebuilding unit; lowest-projected roster in the league in a teardown season.

offense_outlook: O'Connell's play-action scheme around Justin Jefferson faces an open QB competition after adding Kyler Murray to push injury-prone J.J. McCarthy.
defense_outlook: Brian Flores returns to run his aggressive blitz-heavy multiple front, keeping the defense among the league's more disruptive units.

offense_outlook: OC Josh McDaniels returns for Year 2 of continuity with ascending QB Drake Maye, backed by added pass-catchers (Diggs, Doubs) and tackle help.
defense_outlook: Promoted DC Zak Kuhr takes over Vrabel's defense after Terrell Williams shifted to asst HC; scheme continuity is the story.

offense_outlook: HC Kellen Moore calls plays in a heavy two-TE scheme around 2nd-year QB Tyler Shough, upgraded by RB Etienne and LG Edwards.
defense_outlook: Brandon Staley's top-half DVOA defense must replace veterans (Davis, Taylor, possibly Jordan).

offense_outlook: New HC John Harbaugh + OC Matt Nagy build around 2nd-year QB Jaxson Dart, adding pass-catchers Mooney and Likely.
defense_outlook: DC Dennard Wilson's defense is reinforced by LB Tremaine Edmunds and fronted by second-year edge rusher Abdul Carter, but the interior lost anchor DT Dexter Lawrence (traded to Cincinnati for the No. 10 pick), leaving a hole next to the pass rush.

offense_outlook: Frank Reich runs a veteran-led offense with Geno Smith at QB, but a thin WR room and rebuilt O-line cap scoring upside.
defense_outlook: HC Aaron Glenn calls the defense with Minkah Fitzpatrick anchoring; a weak pass rush (26 sacks in 2025) is the concern.

offense_outlook: New OC Sean Mannion inherits Jalen Hurts and an elite O-line in a run-heavy attack; passing-game consistency is the question after a late-2025 slide.
defense_outlook: Vic Fangio's zone defense returns its young secondary (Mitchell, DeJean) plus Riq Woolen, but must replace edge after Jaelan Phillips.

offense_outlook: 1st-year HC Mike McCarthy + OC Brian Angelichio build a West Coast attack around Aaron Rodgers and new WR Michael Pittman Jr.; Rodgers' age is the risk.
defense_outlook: New DC Patrick Graham's unit is fronted by T.J. Watt and Cam Heyward; the rebuilt secondary (Dean, Porter Jr.) is the area to watch.

offense_outlook: New OC Brian Fleury imports a Shanahan-style outside-zone offense for the defending champs; replacing RB Kenneth Walker III is the key question.
defense_outlook: Mike Macdonald's defense (DC Aden Durde) remains the backbone but must absorb edge Boye Mafe and two starting corners.

offense_outlook: Shanahan's wide-zone offense (OC Klay Kubiak) adds WR Mike Evans for Brock Purdy; TE Kittle's Achilles recovery is a key variable.
defense_outlook: New DC Raheem Morris pairs Nick Bosa with trade-add Osa Odighizuwa and returning LB Dre Greenlaw; the front seven is the strength.

offense_outlook: New OC Zac Robinson installs a McVay-branch offense for Baker Mayfield; Chris Godwin as WR1 after Mike Evans is the central question.
defense_outlook: Todd Bowles runs his blitz-heavy defense with added rush (Muhammad, rookie Bain Jr.), but a rebuilt CB room after Jamel Dean is a concern.

offense_outlook: New OC Brian Daboll builds around 2nd-year QB Cam Ward with Wan'Dale Robinson and rookie Carnell Tate; Ward's development is make-or-break.
defense_outlook: New HC Robert Saleh + DC Gus Bradley install an aggressive 4-3 with big investment (Franklin-Myers, Johnson II next to Jeffery Simmons); the D-line is the strength.

offense_outlook: New OC David Blough shifts to a balanced offense using QB Jayden Daniels more under center; a consistent No. 2 WR opposite Terry McLaurin is the need.
defense_outlook: New DC Daronte Jones moves to a 3-4 zone-heavy scheme reinforced by DT Dexter Lawrence and edge Odafe Oweh; the front is the upgrade.
